elements.text: Art of Prompting - Key Elements

content.text:
WHAT
do I want?

WHY / WHO
do I need it?

WHERE
to look?

HOW
do I need it?
Goal (Objective)

What response do you want from Copilot?
(Desired outcome)

Context, Persona &
Audience

Why do you need it?
Who is involved?
Act as a <Persona>
Source (Grounding)
Which information sources should Copilot use (ground against)?
Expectations
How should Copilot respond to meet your expectations?

elements.caption: Key elements of prompting
elements.summary: Grid showing four key elements of prompting: Goal, Context, Source, and Expectations.

elements.text: Art of Prompting - Key Elements

content.text: Generate 3-5 bullet points

to prepare me for a policy 
update meeting with Agency X.

Focus on Email and 
Teams chats since June.

Please use simple language so 
I can get up to speed quickly.

elements.text: What can you do in the document

content.text: Draft with Copilot

– Add content to the Word 
doc

Start from a blank page to create new content 
using
Reference a file

(up to 3 Word or 
PowerPoint files) or providing a topic for 
ungrounded content (ungrounded content will 
not necessarily be factual)
Add new content at any point using

Inspire 
Me

(to build on existing content), referencing 
a file for new grounded content, or providing a 
topic for ungrounded content (ungrounded 
content will not necessarily be factual)
Add suggested image (next to the title)
Put information into a table using

Visualize as a 
table

elements.text: Practice : Create a Copilot training plan

content.text: Go to Copilot in Teams, Word or to
https:///www.microsoft365.com/chat
In chat box write this example prompt below

“Create a training plan to help me with adopting M365 co-pilot. Include a list of 
daily activities that I can do that help me to build Copilot into my daily routine”
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : From Copilot Chat to Word

content.text: Go to a long (preferably complicated) Word document and open Copilot
Enter the following prompt:

“Act as a rewriting expert, your task is to rewrite the following text, making it 
easier to understand for a general audience. The rewritten text should maintain 
the original meaning but should be written in simpler, more accessible language. 
The goal is to ensure that anyone can understand the information. Ensure that the 
text is free from jargon and complicated terms. Suggest a glossary where needed. 
The tone should remain professional but friendly. Reply with Yes if you 
understand”
You can also use Copilot in
Word
to summarize, add content, expand or rewrite particular sections.
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Demo

elements.alt: Microsoft 365 Copilot logo

elements.text: Creating a new Document with Copilot in Word
Practice : Creating a new Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: Go to Word or https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/ word and create a new Blank Document
Enter the following prompt:

“Write a new document on the cost, benefits, and considerations for installing solar panels on homes. Provide an introduction at the beginning of the document and a summary at the end of the document. Include a table of contents”

elements.alt: Screenshot of Microsoft Word showing the Copilot draft box with a generated document about solar panels.

content.text: How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : Creating a new Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: Go to Word or https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/ word and create a new Blank Document
Enter the following prompt:
“Give me a list of questions to ask an agency about a new policy”
or you may enter your own prompt:
“Write a new document about <this topic> and include <these details>.”

elements.alt: Screenshot of Microsoft Word showing the Draft with Copilot text input box.

content.text: How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : Use Existing Documents to Create a new Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: Go to Word or https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/word and create a new Blank Document
Enter a prompt using the forward / to reference existing content:

“Draft a response to the Tailwind RFP using the /<RFP reply framework> to format the response. Reference the /<Boulder Internal Meeting> and the /<Commercial Charger Pro Spec> for technical details and pricing. Include discount details from the following email /<Exciting Opportunity> ”
This is a sample prompt that is using 4 different reference documents.
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

sections.title: Modifying an existing document with Copilot in Word

elements.alt: Microsoft logo

elements.text: Practice : Modifying an existing Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: In an existing Word document (or one used for this practice)
Select and highlight an existing sentence or a paragraph.
Click the Copilot icon on the left of the new line
Enter the following prompt:
“Rewrite this sentence, but make it sound more professional.”
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : Modifying an existing Word document with Copilot in Word

content.text: In an existing Word document (or one used for this practice)
Navigate to the end of the document
In new line
click the Copilot icon on the left of the new line
Click
Inspire Me
or
Keep Writing This
You can regenerate the response more than one time, then you can select the response that you like

elements.alt: Screenshot of Word document with Copilot pane showing questions for Client's Social Media Campaign.

content.text: How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : Visualize Content as a Table

content.text: Go to Word or https://www.microsoft365.com/launch/ word and create a new Blank Document
Enter the following prompt:
“Write a paragraph about the different types of apples.”
Select Keep It and Select the
target content
Click on the
Copilot Icon
to the left of the text and select the “
Visualize as a Table
” option

elements.alt: Screenshot of Word showing a business proposal with Copilot options visible.

content.text: How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

sections.title: Analyzing an existing document with Copilot in Word

elements.text: Practice : Summarize a Document

content.text: Open an existing Word file that you have access to.
At the top of the file, click on the
Copilot Icon
in the ribbon menu and open the Copilot pane on the right side of the document.
Click or enter the prompt:
Summarize the document

elements.alt: Screenshot of Word with Copilot pane summarizing the business proposal document.

content.text: How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Practice : Ask Questions about the Document

content.text: Open an existing Word file that you have access to.
At the top of the file, click on the
Copilot Icon
in the ribbon menu and open the Copilot pane on the right side of the document.
Example Prompt:
“What does this document say about <pick a topic>?”
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.text: Transforming a document to an email with Copilot in Word
Practice : Transform a Document into an Email

content.text: Open an existing Word file that you have access to.
At the top of the
file
, click on the
Copilot Icon
in the ribbon menu and open the Copilot pane on the right side of the document.
Enter the Prompt: “Transform this document into an email”
Copilot will create an email draft based on the document content which can be used to send an email
How did it work? Provide feedback, choose either the thumbs up or down reaction

elements.alt: Screenshot of Microsoft Word showing the Copilot pane generating an email from a business proposal

elements.text: Find more Word prompts to try in Copilot Lab

elements.alt: Copilot logo

content.text:
Write an introduction
Write an intro paragraph for this document and make it sound [professional]

Summarize this doc
Summarize this document [in 3key points]

List pros and cons
List the pros and cons of [the different project ideas mentioned in this document]

Add a paragraph
Add a paragraph that captures [the argument of this document]

Change the font
Change the font to [Segoe UI, 12point]

Create an overview
Create a high-level overview of [agile product development]

Outline a business plan
Draft a business plan outline for a [sustainable marketing company]

Take notes in a meeting
Draft a one-page template that I can use to take notes for a meeting. Include sections for the date and topic

Understand quickly
Explain this document in three sentences

Brainstorm team building ideas
Give me ideas for icebreaker activities for a new team

Improve this document
Give me specific examples from this document on how I can improve it for [a leadership review?]

Write more confidently
How can I more concisely describe [time management]?
